refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is already focused on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It is solution-neutral as it does not specify how to achieve this, only what is required. Therefore, it is atomic and does not need further decomposition.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission mechanisms to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a network quality monitoring and control system that dynamically adjusts transmission parameters to meet packet loss targets
Use a redundant communication path strategy to ensure packet delivery meets the loss criteria
Incorporate a buffering and smoothing mechanism at the receiver to compensate for packet loss and maintain teleoperation quality
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission mechanisms to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Tight coupling between error correction and retransmission logic if not modularized
Dependency on network condition monitoring for adaptive adjustments
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically separates the communication protocol design from physical network implementation, supporting independence. Adaptive mechanisms can be modularized to reduce coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters and stable enough for adaptive protocols to be effective
Risk that extreme network degradation may exceed protocol capabilities leading to higher packet loss
Assumes that safety policies allow for retransmission delays without compromising teleoperation safety
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including a network condition monitoring DP to support adaptive protocol adjustments and further reduce coupling between functional elements.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the network communication stack and protocol management components responsible for handling data transmission, including adaptive error correction and retransmission mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional interface for sending and receiving data packets over the network, essential for implementing communication protocols.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outputs processed data and status information after communication handling.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the communication protocol logically belongs here; minimal diffusion since protocol logic is centralized.
Vehicle module uses communication but does not implement protocol logic; would cause high diffusion if protocol implemented here.
Teleoperation depends on communication but is not responsible for protocol implementation; poor containment.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, not communication protocol; very poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission, as it directly manages network communication functions and can contain the DP with high cohesion and low diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on lower-level hardware or network drivers outside NGHE Solution may cause some diffusion.
If adaptive mechanisms require real-time data from Vehicle or Teleoperation modules, interface complexity may increase.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission mechanisms to maintain packet loss within the customer-defined limit, thus fulfilling the functional requirement.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on lower-level hardware or network drivers outside NGHE Solution may cause some diffusion.
If adaptive mechanisms require real-time data from Vehicle or Teleoperation modules, interface complexity may increase.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2), indicating that the DP is well centralized within this IM boundary. Other modules have poor containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module manages the communication stack and can be tested against the packet loss acceptance criteria (≤1% under agreed network conditions).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and logical, as this module encompasses the communication protocol implementation necessary to meet the FR.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define interfaces and data exchange protocols with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to minimize interface complexity and diffusion risk.
Assess and document dependencies on lower-level hardware or network drivers to manage potential diffusion outside the NGHE Solution module.
Include verification plans within the NGHE Solution module to ensure packet loss targets are met under operational conditions.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is well addressed by the design parameter and implementation module. The NGHE Solution module effectively contains the DP with minimal diffusion risk, and verification feasibility is confirmed. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to avoid unnecessary complexity by not decomposing further unless new requirements emerge.

revise.why: To address recommended revisions, explicitly define interfaces and data exchange protocols with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, and document dependencies on lower-level hardware or network drivers to minimize diffusion risk and ensure verification plans are included.

assumptions: The current FR fully captures the communication protocol requirements for packet loss control.
The NGHE Solution module is capable of managing the communication stack and verification activities.
Dependencies on external hardware and network drivers can be managed through interface definitions.
open_risks: Potential diffusion due to dependencies on lower-level hardware or network drivers outside NGHE Solution.
Interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may increase due to adaptive mechanisms.
evidence_needed: Verification test results demonstrating packet loss ≤1% under agreed network conditions.
Documentation of interface definitions and dependency assessments.
Verification plans within NGHE Solution module.
